title: Sole traders
summary: 9706 P2 — income statements and statements of financial position for sole traders.
learningObjectives: Prepare statement of profit or loss for sole trader.
Calculate gross profit and profit for the year.

simpleExplanation.title: Accounts for one owner
simpleExplanation.summary: Sole trader accounts show trading profit, expenses, and net profit — the SOFP lists assets, liabilities, and owner's capital minus drawings.
steps: Trading account — sales − COGS = gross profit.
SPL — gross profit − expenses = net profit.
Drawings — reduce capital, not an expense.

flashcards.back: Reduce capital on SOFP — never an expense on SPL.
Revenue → COGS → Gross profit → Expenses → Net profit.
Single owner — all profit belongs to them.

sections.content: **Sole traders** (1.5.2) — use **vertical format** with clear **gross → net profit**. Capital section must reconcile opening, profit, drawings, closing.

sections.question: Extract: Sales $120,000; opening inventory $8,000; purchases $62,000; closing inventory $10,000; expenses $28,000; capital opening $45,000; drawings $12,000. Calculate gross profit, net profit, and closing capital.

sections.label: Mark a sole trader accounts question
